title: Generation Models Know Space: Unleashing Implicit 3D Priors for Scene Understanding
task: 3D scene understanding and spatial reasoning for MLLMs
key_results: Outperforms state-of-the-art on 3D scene understanding, spatial reasoning, and embodied manipulation benchmarks without explicit 3D supervision
comments: Clever repurposing of video diffusion models' implicit spatial priors; demonstrates that generative models learn robust physical priors as a byproduct of temporal coherence.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Video generation models learn implicit 3D structural priors from temporal coherence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; extracted spatiotemporal features improve MLLM spatial reasoning

title: SAMA: Factorized Semantic Anchoring and Motion Alignment for Instruction-Guided Video Editing
task: Instruction-guided video editing with semantic-motion factorization
key_results: Achieves state-of-the-art among open-source and competitive with commercial systems; factorized pre-training alone yields strong zero-shot editing
comments: The factorization paradigm elegantly separates semantic understanding from temporal dynamics, avoiding explicit external priors that limit generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Decoupling semantic anchoring and motion modeling improves editing quality
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; zero-shot ability validates the factorization approach

title: FASTER: Rethinking Real-Time Flow VLAs
task: Real-time vision-language-action inference with low latency
key_results: Compresses immediate reaction denoising tenfold into single step; enables real-time robotics on consumer GPUs with table-tennis task validation
comments: Addresses a practical bottleneck in VLA deployment; Horizon-Aware Schedule is elegant but relies on action chunking assumptions.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Constant sampling schedules are inefficient for reaction latency in flow-based VLAs
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; adaptive prioritization of near-term actions reduces reaction latency

title: 3DreamBooth: High-Fidelity 3D Subject-Driven Video Generation Model
task: 3D-aware subject-driven video generation
key_results: Achieves view-consistent video generation of customized 3D subjects via 1-frame optimization; enables multi-view adaptation without exhaustive video training
comments: Novel insight that single-frame optimization with geometric conditioning can avoid temporal overfitting; asymmetrical multi-view strategy is well-motivated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Restricting spatial updates in 1-frame optimization prevents temporal overfitting
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; robust 3D priors learned without multi-view video data

title: Bridging Semantic and Kinematic Conditions with Diffusion-based Discrete Motion Tokenizer
task: Human motion generation with semantic and kinematic control
key_results: Reduces trajectory error from 0.72 to 0.08 cm and FID from 0.083 to 0.029 on HumanML3D using one-sixth of tokens; improves under stronger constraints
comments: Bridging discrete tokens and continuous diffusion is elegant; the three-stage framework cleanly separates concerns.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Delegating motion recovery to diffusion decoder enables compact tokens
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; single-layer tokens preserve fidelity with 83% token reduction

title: MonoArt: Progressive Structural Reasoning for Monocular Articulated 3D Reconstruction
task: Monocular articulated 3D object reconstruction
key_results: State-of-the-art on PartNet-Mobility in reconstruction accuracy and inference speed; generalizes to robotic manipulation and articulated scenes
comments: Progressive structural reasoning avoids direct articulation regression; missing quantitative results and comparison metrics in abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Progressive transformation into canonical geometry enables stable articulation inference
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; outperforms multi-view and retrieval-based baselines

title: LVOmniBench: Pioneering Long Audio-Video Understanding Evaluation for Omnimodal LLMs
task: Evaluating omnimodal LLMs on long-form audio-video understanding
key_results: Gemini 3 Pro achieves ~65% accuracy; open-source models <35%; reveals challenges in long-term memory, temporal localization, and multimodal perception
comments: Critical gap identified between short-clip and real-world long-form video; benchmark is valuable but shows existing models are far from production-ready.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current OmniLLMs struggle with long-form audio-visual understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; significant accuracy gaps across all tested models

title: Cubic Discrete Diffusion: Discrete Visual Generation on High-Dimensional Representation Tokens
task: Discrete visual generation on high-dimensional representation tokens
key_results: First discrete generation model for high-dimensional (768-1024 dim) tokens; state-of-the-art on ImageNet-256 with strong scaling (900M to 3.7B); validates discretized tokens preserve original capabilities
comments: Enabling unified multimodal architectures via discrete high-dim tokens is ambitious; fine-grained masking approach is well-designed.

hypotheses.hypothesis: High-dimensional discrete tokens can preserve representation capabilities for both understanding and generation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; same tokens effective for dual tasks

title: Memento-Skills: Let Agents Design Agents
task: Continual agent self-improvement via skill design
key_results: 26.2% and 116.2% relative accuracy improvements on General AI Assistants and Humanity's Last Exam; agent autonomously designs agents for new tasks
comments: Memory-based RL with externalised skills enables parameter-free adaptation; impressive but lacks analysis of failure modes.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Agents can autonomously design task-specific agents through iterative skill refinement
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; sustained gains across benchmarks

title: F2LLM-v2: Inclusive, Performant, and Efficient Embeddings for a Multilingual World
task: Multilingual embedding models for 200+ languages
key_results: F2LLM-v2-14B ranks first on 11 MTEB benchmarks; smaller models achieve SOTA for resource-constrained settings; supports 200+ languages including low-resource
comments: Matryoshka learning + pruning + distillation is effective recipe; emphasis on underserved languages is important.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Two-stage LLM-based training with matryoshka learning enables efficient multilingual embeddings
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA across diverse model sizes

title: Nemotron-Cascade 2: Post-Training LLMs with Cascade RL and Multi-Domain On-Policy Distillation
task: Post-training compact MoE LLMs with cascade RL
key_results: Nemotron-Cascade 2 (30B MoE, 3B active) achieves Gold in IMO, IOI, ICPC with 20x fewer parameters than DeepSeekV3.2; competitive with frontier models
comments: Multi-domain on-policy distillation during RL is clever for recovering regressions; exceptional density-to-capability ratio.

model_io.model: Nemotron-Cascade 2 (MoE with cascade RL + multi-domain distillation)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cascade RL + multi-domain on-policy distillation sustains gains in compact MoE models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; strong reasoning with high parameter efficiency

title: Cognitive Mismatch in Multimodal Large Language Models for Discrete Symbol Understanding
task: Evaluating MLLMs on discrete symbol understanding
key_results: Models fail at basic symbol recognition but succeed at complex reasoning; open-source models <35%, frontier models ~65-75%; reveals cognitive mismatch
comments: Uncovers fundamental limitation: MLLMs rely on linguistic probability rather than true visual perception of symbols; important diagnostic.

hypotheses.hypothesis: MLLMs exhibit cognitive mismatch in discrete symbol understanding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; fail basic recognition but succeed at complex reasoning

title: EffectErase: Joint Video Object Removal and Insertion for High-Quality Effect Erasing
task: Video object removal with effect erasing and consistent background synthesis
key_results: VOR dataset: 60K paired videos, 5 effect types; EffectErase achieves superior performance in effect-aware removal via reciprocal insertion-removal learning
comments: Dataset addresses real gap in video inpainting; treating insertion as auxiliary task is well-motivated for learning complementary behaviors.

outputs: video with object removed and background restored

hypotheses.hypothesis: Insertion as auxiliary task improves removal quality through complementary learning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; insertion-removal consistency objective enhances effect erasing

title: VTC-Bench: Evaluating Agentic Multimodal Models via Compositional Visual Tool Chaining
task: Evaluating agentic MLLMs on tool composition and visual reasoning
key_results: Gemini-3.0-Pro achieves 51% on VTC-Bench; reveals struggles in tool generalization and multi-tool composition; 680 problems across 9-category hierarchy
comments: Comprehensive benchmark with realistic OpenCV tools; exposes that models rely on familiar subsets rather than selecting optimal tools.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Current MLLMs struggle with generalization to diverse tool-sets and multi-tool composition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; limited to familiar tools, inefficient planning

title: MOSS-TTS Technical Report
task: Speech generation with token-based autoregressive modeling
key_results: Supports zero-shot voice cloning, token-level duration control, pronunciation control, code-switching, long-form generation; two model variants for scalability vs. efficiency
comments: Discrete audio tokens + causal tokenizer is sound recipe; variable-bitrate RVQ for semantic-acoustic coupling is well-designed.

model_io.model: MOSS-TTS (autoregressive speech generator on discrete tokens)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Causal tokenizer with variable-bitrate RVQ enables unified semantic-acoustic representation for zero-shot voice cloning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; supports multilingual and long-form generation

title: OSM-based Domain Adaptation for Remote Sensing VLMs
task: Domain adaptation of VLMs for remote sensing without teacher models
key_results: OSMDA-VLM achieves SOTA on 10 benchmarks; eliminates dependency on frontier teacher models; generates annotations via OSM pairing without manual labels
comments: Self-contained framework using OSM as annotation engine is clever and scalable; demonstrates that auxiliary geographic data replaces need for stronger teachers.

hypotheses.hypothesis: OSM-based pseudo-labeling enables domain adaptation without external teacher models
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; achieves SOTA with lower training cost

title: ProRL Agent: Rollout-as-a-Service for RL Training of Multi-Turn LLM Agents
task: Scalable rollout infrastructure for RL training of multi-turn LLM agents
key_results: ProRL Agent enables RL training on software engineering, math, STEM, and coding; supports rootless HPC environments; rollout-as-a-service design decouples orchestration
comments: Infrastructure contribution; clean separation of concerns (rollout vs. training) improves maintainability but limited novelty in RL itself.

model_io.model: ProRL Agent (RL infrastructure for agentic rollouts)

title: Loc3R-VLM: Language-based Localization and 3D Reasoning with Vision-Language Models
task: Language-based localization and 3D reasoning with monocular video
key_results: SOTA on language-based localization and 3D QA benchmarks; outperforms 2D and video baselines via global layout + egocentric perspective supervision
comments: Joint objectives for layout reconstruction and situation modeling elegantly ground language in 3D; lightweight camera pose priors from foundation models is practical.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Global layout + egocentric supervision enables VLMs to reason about 3D space
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; SOTA on localization and 3D QA tasks

title: MHPO: Modulated Hazard-aware Policy Optimization for Stable Reinforcement Learning
task: Stable reinforcement learning with hazard-aware policy optimization
key_results: MHPO outperforms existing methods on reasoning benchmarks (text and vision-language); Log-Fidelity Modulator enables differentiable ratio control; asymmetric hazard penalties prevent mode collapse
comments: Survival analysis for hazard modeling is creative; addresses real instability issues in GRPO but lacks comparison with recent PPO variants.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Log-Fidelity Modulator + Decoupled Hazard Penalty improve training stability and prevent mode collapse
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent gains on diverse benchmarks

title: Reasoning over mathematical objects: on-policy reward modeling and test time aggregation
task: Mathematical reasoning with formal object derivation and test-time aggregation
key_results: Strong LMs (Qwen3-235B, o3) struggle on Principia; on-policy judge training boosts performance; test-time aggregation scales compute; cross-format generalization to numerical/MCQA
comments: Principia benchmark addresses real gap in formal reasoning; on-policy judge training is well-motivated but evaluation limited to few models.

hypotheses.hypothesis: On-policy judge training improves mathematical object reasoning
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; boosts performance on Principia and numerical tasks

title: Prompt-Free Universal Region Proposal Network
task: Prompt-free universal object proposal generation
key_results: Works with 5% of COCO data; generalizes to 19 datasets without fine-tuning (underwater, industrial defect, remote sensing); no external prompts required
comments: Learnable query embeddings with visual feature coupling and cascade self-prompting is effective; impressive zero-shot generalization.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Self-prompted learnable embeddings enable prompt-free object detection generalization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; zero-shot transfer across domains

title: Mending the Holes: Mitigating Reward Hacking in Reinforcement Learning for Multilingual Translation
task: Low-resource multilingual machine translation via RL with quality estimation
key_results: WALAR improves 1400 language directions on Flores-101; outperforms LLaMAX; retains high-resource performance while elevating low-resource translation
comments: Insight about QE model failure modes ('holes') motivating word/language alignment is practical; RL-only approach using monolingual data is resource-efficient.

model_io.model: LLM with RL (QE-based reward, word/language alignment)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mitigating QE model holes via word/language alignment improves low-resource translation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; large gains on 1400 Flores-101 directions

title: Matryoshka Gaussian Splatting
task: Level-of-detail rendering for 3D Gaussian Splatting
key_results: Continuous LoD without sacrificing full-capacity quality; stochastic budget training maintains backbone performance; smooth speed-quality trade-off from single model
comments: Clever stochastic budget training enables continuous LoD without architecture changes; useful for practical 3D applications.

model_io.model: MGS (ordered Gaussian splatting with stochastic training)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Stochastic budget training enables continuous LoD without quality degradation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; matches full-capacity performance across budgets

title: COT-FM: Cluster-wise Optimal Transport Flow Matching
task: Faster flow matching via cluster-wise optimal transport
key_results: COT-FM accelerates sampling and improves generation quality; straightens vector fields via cluster-wise coupling; plug-and-play across 2D, image, and robotics tasks
comments: Cluster-wise OT to improve flow straightness is intuitive; no architectural changes required, making adoption practical.

model_io.model: COT-FM (flow matching with optimal transport reshaping)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cluster-wise OT yields straighter vector fields and faster convergence
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; consistent acceleration and quality improvement

title: Structural basis for prostaglandin and drug transport via SLCO2A1
task: Determine prostaglandin and NSAID substrate selection and transport mechanism in SLCO2A1
key_results: Cryo-EM structures of SLCO2A1 bound to endogenous prostaglandins and four NSAIDs reveal molecular basis for substrate recognition; MD simulations elucidate substrate selection specificity
comments: Structures of drug-bound states provide valuable mechanistic detail for rational drug design, though cellular penetration kinetics and in vivo bioavailability remain incompletely characterized.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Substrate selection within SLCO superfamily depends on distinct molecular recognition features
hypotheses.result: Structures reveal specific binding pocket residues and ligand-protein contacts that rationalize PG and NSAID selectivity

title: Structural mechanism of substrate binding and inhibition of human taurine transporter
task: Determine substrate binding and inhibition mechanism of human taurine transporter
key_results: Taurine, β-alanine, and cyclic P4S inhibitor all bind at the central substrate-binding site; inward-open conformation with TM1a bending facilitates intracellular gate opening
comments: High-resolution structural snapshots provide clear mechanistic insight, though dynamic conformational transitions during actual transport remain partially unexplored.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Taurine binds at a unique central site distinct from other ligands
hypotheses.result: All tested ligands (taurine, β-alanine, P4S) occupy the same substrate-binding site

title: A single cluster of RNA Polymerase II molecules is stably associated with active genes
task: Characterize RNAPII cluster formation, stability, and functional association with active genes during transcription
key_results: RNAPII clusters are stably associated with active gene loci; cluster formation depends on transcription initiation; cluster intensity strongly correlates with transcriptional output; clusters reflect local polymerase accumulation, not phase separation
comments: Single-molecule tracking data compellingly refute the phase separation hypothesis, though the precise molecular basis of cluster stability and its evolutionary advantage remain open questions.

model_io.model: Drosophila melanogaster embryo during Zygotic Genome Activation

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNAPII clusters form via phase separation or higher-order assembly mechanisms
hypotheses.result: Clusters reflect local accumulation of transcriptionally engaged polymerases; phase separation is not required

title: Soft bionic actuation explains the functional role of whisking in seal whisker sensing
task: Elucidate the functional role of whisking in hydrodynamic sensing by seal whiskers
key_results: Undulated seal whiskers show 3-fold lower vortex-induced vibrations and >50-fold higher SNR than sea lion whiskers; active whisking is essential for prey tracking
comments: Elegant bionic integration of natural whiskers with electrohydraulic actuators provides excellent experimental control, though field validation in natural hunting behavior remains limited.

model_io.model: Natural harbor seal whisker + electrohydraulic soft actuator
Bionic seal muzzle with 30 whiskers per side

hypotheses.hypothesis: Undulatory morphology alone suppresses self-induced noise
hypotheses.result: Seal whiskers show threefold lower VIV; morphology is necessary but active whisking further enhances SNR

title: A computational analysis of biophysical and geometric constraints refutes existing hypotheses of cross-excitation in dorsal root ganglia
task: Identify biophysical mechanisms underlying cross-excitation in dorsal root ganglia
key_results: Existing hypotheses (glial sheath interactions, neuron-neuron coupling) are individually and collectively insufficient to explain cross-excitation in normal conditions
comments: The paper identifies what is NOT responsible but leaves the true mechanism unresolved, highlighting a genuine gap in sensory neuroscience.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Classical neuron–neuron interactions through glial sheath gaps explain cross-excitation
hypotheses.result: Refuted; neither individual mechanisms nor their combinations adequately describe phenomenon

title: Loss of Pum2 exacerbates colitis by disrupting macrophage–epithelial crosstalk and promoting epithelial necroptosis
task: Identify Pum2 as regulator of intestinal inflammation via macrophage-epithelial crosstalk
key_results: Pum2 expression reduced in UC patient colonic tissue, inversely correlating with disease activity; Pum2 deficiency exacerbated DSS-induced colitis with heightened macrophage inflammation and TNFα-dependent epithelial necroptosis; dynamic downregulation occurs during active inflammation
comments: The identification of Pum2 as a macrophage-epithelial crosstalk regulator is novel, though the direct molecular targets of Pum2 RNA-binding activity in this context require characterization.

model_io.model: UC patient colonic tissues; DSS-induced colitis; Pum2-deficient mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reduced Pum2 expression in UC correlates with disease activity
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; inverse correlation between Pum2 levels and disease activity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pum2 deficiency exacerbates colitis by promoting macrophage hyperactivation and TNFα-dependent epithelial necroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Pum2 loss drives heightened inflammation and barrier breakdown

title: A cell cycle-dependent transition of acetylation to phosphorylation regulates timely centrosome maturation
task: Characterize mechanism controlling timely centrosome maturation during G2/M phase
key_results: CDK1 phosphorylates RNF40 (T529/T557) enabling PLK1 recruitment; acetylation-to-phosphorylation transition of RNF40 in late G2/M is required for centrosome maturation and bipolar spindle assembly
comments: The acetylation-to-phosphorylation switch is an elegant post-translational regulation mechanism for cell cycle control, though the acetylase/deacetylase involved remain unidentified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CDK1-dependent RNF40 phosphorylation primes PLK1 binding
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; phosphorylation at T529/T557 promotes PLK1 recruitment

hypotheses.hypothesis: RNF40 acetylation inhibits centrosome maturation in interphase
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; constitutive acetylation impairs PLK1 localization and causes mitotic catastrophe

title: AP-1 mediates cellular adaptation and memory formation
task: Demonstrate AP-1-mediated cellular memory formation during development of drug resistance
key_results: Cancer cells form AP-1-dependent cellular memories upon low-dose therapy exposure; adaptation enables resistance to high-dose therapy; memories are encoded in cis as chromatin accessibility changes; encoding occurs for both transiently induced and stochastically arising expression
comments: The cis-encoding of cellular memory through AP-1-dependent chromatin accessibility is an elegant example of epigenetic learning, though the stability and reversibility of these memories over extended timeframes warrant investigation.

model_io.model: Cancer cells with AP-1 two-color reporter system

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cancer cells encode transient therapy-induced gene expression into stable cellular memory via AP-1
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; AP-1-dependent cis-encoded chromatin accessibility maintains resistance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cellular memories include both transcriptionally induced and stochastically arising expression patterns
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; memories encode both deterministic and probabilistic expression states

title: Mitorubin, berberrubine-based compounds that improve mitochondrial function, exhibit cardioprotective effects against age-related cardiac dysfunction
task: Develop mitochondrial activators targeting MITOL for age-related cardiac dysfunction
key_results: Berberrubine-based compounds (Mitorubin) increased MITOL expression, mitochondrial DNA content, and respiration; oral administration restored mitochondrial function, improved cardiac performance, suppressed hypertrophy, alleviated pulmonary congestion; extended lifespan in high-fat diet mice
comments: The water-soluble derivative represents a practical formulation improvement, though long-term safety in aging populations requires additional investigation.

model_io.model: Cultured cardiomyocytes; age-related cardiac dysfunction model; high-fat diet mice

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mitorubin improves cardiac function by promoting MITOL expression and mitochondrial activation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Mitorubin increased MITOL, mitochondrial proteins, and cardiac performance

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mitorubin is safe under chronic administration
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; did not shorten lifespan in aged mice; extended lifespan in HFD mice

title: Dielectric double shell characterization of yeast cells exposed to simulated microgravity
task: Characterize microgravity effects on yeast cellular properties using dielectrophoresis
key_results: Simulated microgravity (2D-clinostat) induced morphological and membrane changes in S. cerevisiae; reduced membrane folding factor impaired nutrient uptake and energy production; dielectric analysis revealed decreased membrane permittivity and conductivity
comments: Dielectrophoresis provides novel biophysical insights into microgravity effects, though the functional consequences of altered membrane properties on long-term viability and metabolic capacity require further investigation.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Microgravity alters yeast cell membrane properties and morphology
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced folding factor and altered membrane permittivity/conductivity observed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Microgravity-induced membrane changes impair nutrient uptake and energy production
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; reduced folding factor correlates with impaired cellular metabolism

title: Visualizing suborganellar lipid distribution using correlative light and electron microscopy
task: Develop correlative light and electron microscopy (Lipid-CLEM) workflow for nanoscale lipid visualization
key_results: Lipid-CLEM enables quantification of relative lipid densities in membrane nanodomains; differential sphingomyelin partitioning observed in early endosome intraluminal vesicles, recycling tubules, and boundary membranes; nanoscale lipid organization previously only documented for proteins
comments: The Lipid-CLEM method is a significant technical advance for spatial lipidomics, though validation of lipid-specific labeling fidelity and potential click chemistry artifacts in membranes should be comprehensively documented.

model_io.model: Cultured cells imaged by correlative light and electron microscopy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipid-CLEM enables nanoscale visualization of lipid organization in membrane nanodomains
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; differential sphingomyelin partitioning quantified across early endosome subcompartments

hypotheses.hypothesis: Lipids organize into nanoscale domains at cellular membranes
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; sphingomyelin enrichment observed in specific endosomal microdomains

title: Melanin concentrating hormone regulates bone cell activities and calcium metabolism in regenerating goldfish scales
task: Characterize MCH regulation of bone cell activities and calcium metabolism in regenerating scales
key_results: Single MCH treatment suppressed osteoclast-associated genes, decreased plasma calcium and TRAP activity; continuous MCH elevated both osteoblast (ALP) and osteoclast (TRAP) markers but decreased scale calcium; MCH receptor 2 involved in calcification regulation
comments: The biphasic response to single versus continuous MCH administration suggests complex feedback mechanisms in bone remodeling that may involve receptor desensitization or secondary signaling changes.

model_io.model: Cultured goldfish scales; goldfish in vivo

hypotheses.hypothesis: Single MCH treatment inhibits osteoclast activity and reduces plasma calcium
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; MCH suppressed osteoclast-associated genes and TRAP activity

hypotheses.hypothesis: Continuous MCH treatment coordinately affects osteoblasts and osteoclasts, decreasing scale calcification
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; prolonged MCH elevated both ALP and TRAP but reduced scale calcium

title: Hypoxia-induced gene expression changes in N. vectensis embryos
task: Characterize hypoxia-induced gene expression responses in early metazoan embryos
key_results: N. vectensis hypoxia induces reversible developmental arrest; transcriptomic profiling reveals conserved hypoxia-responsive genes and pathways shared with bilaterians; core HIF-dependent toolkit established in cnidarian–bilaterian ancestor
comments: Using a representative early-diverging metazoan strengthens evolutionary inferences, though the ecological relevance of O2 variability to Neoproterozoic environments could be better quantified.

hypotheses.hypothesis: N. vectensis embryogenesis is O2-dependent with reversible hypoxic arrest
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; hypoxia induces reversible developmental arrest

hypotheses.hypothesis: Embryonic hypoxia response toolkit is conserved between cnidarians and bilaterians
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; core hypoxia-responsive genes and pathways identified in N. vectensis

title: Thioredoxin protects against diabetic hearing loss by regulating TOMM22 mediated mitochondrial autophagy in hair cells and inhibiting microglial M1 polarization
task: Elucidate thioredoxin protection against diabetic hearing loss via mitochondrial and microglial mechanisms
key_results: Trx1 overexpression inhibited PINK1/Parkin-mediated mitophagy via TOMM22 maintenance, increased mitochondrial membrane potential, reduced apoptosis; drove microglia M2 polarization; elevated serum Trx associated with decreased OAE parameters
comments: The dual mechanism (cell-autonomous mitochondrial homeostasis and non-autonomous microglial polarization) is well-supported, though the counterintuitive clinical association between elevated Trx and hearing loss requires careful interpretation.

model_io.model: Diabetic mouse cochlea; HEI-OC1 hair cells; BV-2 microglia

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trx1 protects hair cells by suppressing PINK1/Parkin-mediated mitophagy via TOMM22
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; TOMM22 inhibition reversed Trx1 protection

hypotheses.hypothesis: Trx1 indirectly protects hair cells through microglial M2 polarization
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; conditioned medium from Trx1-treated microglia protected hair cells

title: Collagen-specific molecular chaperone Hsp47 in inguinal white adipose tissue promotes high-fat diet-induced inflammatory gene expression in male mice
task: Determine depot-specific role of Hsp47 in adipose inflammation during high-fat diet
key_results: Hsp47 mRNA/protein increased in ingWAT after 12-week HFD; adipocyte-specific Hsp47 knockout reduced adipocyte size, fibrosis markers, and pro-inflammatory genes specifically in ingWAT with minimal epiWAT effect
comments: The depot-specific differences suggest distinct mechanical or metabolic properties between inguinal and epididymal fat, warranting further investigation of tissue-intrinsic factors.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hsp47 contributes to HFD-induced adipose dysfunction via collagen-integrin signaling
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Hsp47 aKO reduces inflammation and fibrosis in ingWAT

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hsp47 effects are depot-independent
hypotheses.result: Refuted; minimal effects observed in epiWAT compared to ingWAT

title: ROS-NLRP3 participates in the pyroptosis response of excretory-secretory products from protoscoleces of Echinococcus granulosus in hepatocytes
task: Determine mechanism of hepatocyte damage in cystic echinococcosis via NLRP3-mediated pyroptosis
key_results: E. granulosus excretory-secretory products (ESPs) induced hepatocyte pyroptosis via ROS-NLRP3 inflammasome pathway; NLRP3 inhibition (MCC950) and ROS scavenging (NAC) markedly attenuated ESP-induced cytotoxicity and pyroptosis
comments: The ROS-NLRP3 mechanism is well-established in inflammatory pathologies, though identification of specific parasite-derived DAMP(s) triggering inflammasome activation would strengthen mechanistic understanding.

model_io.model: AML-12 hepatocytes exposed to E. granulosus ESPs

hypotheses.hypothesis: ESP-induced hepatocyte damage occurs via NLRP3 inflammasome-dependent pyroptosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; NLRP3 inhibition markedly reduced cytotoxicity and pyroptosis

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pyroptosis is ROS-dependent
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; ROS scavenger NAC suppressed pyroptosis and inflammasome activation

title: The redundant protein synthesis gene Aimp1 challenges the canonical inverse relationship between translation and autophagy
task: Identify redundant protein synthesis genes that regulate autophagy independently of mTORC1
key_results: Aimp1, a non-essential multi-aminoacyl tRNA synthetase complex member, limits autophagy by uncoupling mTORC1 activity while minimally affecting protein synthesis; Aimp1 depletion impairs innate immunity kinetics in myeloid cells
comments: The challenge to the canonical inverse relationship between translation and autophagy is valuable, though the immunological phenotype suggests Aimp1 has broader roles beyond the protein synthesis-autophagy axis.

model_io.model: Mammalian cells; murine myeloid cells; Aimp1 knockout models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aimp1 functions redundantly for protein synthesis but uniquely limits autophagy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Aimp1 depletion minimally affects translation but enhances autophagy

hypotheses.hypothesis: Aimp1 uncouples mTORC1 from autophagy suppression
hypotheses.result: Confirmed; Aimp1 depletion reduces mTORC1-mediated autophagy inhibition

title: International testing and refinement of AI algorithms predicting acute leukemia subtypes from routine laboratory data
task: International validation and refinement of AI for acute leukemia subtype prediction from routine laboratory data
key_results: Pretrained model achieved AUROC 0.94 (AML), 0.98 (APL), 0.84 (ALL) with high confidence cutoff; ensemble (Isolation Forest + LOF) improved AML AUROC to 0.84 for below-threshold patients with only 12.1% exclusion
comments: Large diverse international cohort (6206 patients, 20 centers) strengthens generalizability; ensemble approach addressing confident-threshold exclusion is pragmatic but reflects model uncertainty rather than robustness.

model_io.model: Pretrained neural network + ensemble (Isolation Forest + Local Outlier Factor)
inputs: routine laboratory results (complete blood count, biochemistry)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Pretrained AI model generalizes across international centers for leukemia diagnosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with high AUROC for AML and APL; partial for ALL

hypotheses.hypothesis: Ensemble anomaly detection improves predictions for below-threshold patients
hypotheses.result: Confirmed, improving AML AUROC while reducing exclusion rate

title: Ultra-precision deconvolution of spatial transcriptomics decodes immune heterogeneity and fate-defining programs in tissues
task: Spatial transcriptomics deconvolution to map immune cell subpopulations
key_results: UCASpatial identified low-abundant cell subpopulations in colorectal cancer and revealed IL11-IL11RA signaling drives pro-fibrotic community in wound healing
comments: Entropy-based weighting is a novel technical contribution, though biological validation is limited to wound healing and one cancer type.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Chromosome 20q gain excludes T cells through HERV-H silencing and impaired interferon response
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in colorectal cancer samples

hypotheses.hypothesis: IL11-IL11RA signaling in stromal-immune triad drives fibrosis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in C57BL/6 wound healing model

title: A disease-agnostic approach to ensemble learning for infectious disease forecasting
task: Disease-agnostic ensemble forecasting for infectious disease outbreaks
key_results: epiFFORMA outperformed equal-weighting and individual models on 10 diseases; 23–31% improvement in some metrics
comments: Synthetic data-driven weighting is elegant for emerging diseases, but real-time performance on novel pathogens remains unvalidated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Weights derived from synthetic epidemiological dynamics improve forecasts without historical disease data
hypotheses.result: Confirmed across 10 infectious diseases

title: Intrinsic mechanisms and microenvironmental cues fine-tune plasticity of esophageal progenitors
task: Elucidation of regulatory mechanisms controlling esophageal progenitor plasticity and metaplasia susceptibility
key_results: Sox9 induction via Hedgehog and TGF-β/BMP signaling promotes squamo-columnar junction progenitor state; Cox-2 modulates plasticity through epithelial cell-intrinsic mechanism
comments: Multi-level mechanistic insight (epithelial-stromal cross-talk plus cell-intrinsic regulation) strengthens the model; Cox-2 inhibitor repurposing is clinically promising but awaits in vivo validation.

model_io.model: Genetic and pharmacological perturbation in mouse and human esophageal models

hypotheses.hypothesis: Hedgehog signaling indirectly induces Sox9 via epithelial-stromal communication and directly via cell-autonomous mechanisms
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in mouse and human models

hypotheses.hypothesis: TGF-β and BMP pathways synergistically induce Sox9 and promote metaplasia-predisposed state
hypotheses.result: Confirmed

hypotheses.hypothesis: Cox-2 inhibition suppresses Hedgehog-induced Sox9 through epithelial cell-intrinsic effects
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in both mouse and human models

title: Generalist biological artificial intelligence in modeling the language of life
task: Framework and roadmap for generalist biological AI across DNA, RNA, protein, and cellular systems
key_results: Synthesized advances in foundation models for molecular sequence and structure; identified opportunities in synergizing language and structural AI, autonomous discovery agents, and virtual cell integration
comments: Comprehensive review articulating the vision of multimodal biological AI; challenges (data, complexity, validation) are identified but solutions remain largely programmatic rather than algorithmic.

title: Open and sustainable AI: challenges, opportunities and the road ahead in the life sciences
task: Framework development for open and sustainable AI in life sciences
key_results: Mapped 300+ ecosystem components and provided implementation pathways for reproducible AI
comments: A perspective piece identifying systemic challenges rather than providing algorithmic innovations; focuses on governance and best practices.

title: LazySlide: accessible and interoperable whole-slide image analysis
task: Whole-slide image analysis integration with omics workflows
key_results: LazySlide enables tissue/cell segmentation, feature extraction, and zero-shot classification via vision-language models
comments: Leverages existing foundation models rather than novel methodology; value lies in workflow integration and scverse compatibility.

title: Backpropagation-free spiking neural networks with the forward–forward algorithm
task: Training spiking neural networks via forward-forward algorithm without backpropagation
key_results: FF-SNN achieved accuracy comparable to backpropagation-trained SNNs on static datasets; outperformed on complex spiking task SHD with lighter architecture
comments: Forward-forward training offers biological plausibility and hardware efficiency, but scalability to large-scale tasks and true neuromorphic hardware deployment remain undemonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Forward-forward algorithm trains SNNs comparably to backpropagation on static data
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with competitive accuracy

hypotheses.hypothesis: FF-SNN outperforms backpropagation SNNs on complex spiking tasks
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on SHD dataset

title: Meta-analysis, WGCNA, and machine learning converge on a four-gene biomarker panel for heat stress tolerance in Solanum lycopersicum
task: Identification of heat-stress tolerance biomarker panel in tomato via meta-analysis and machine learning
key_results: Four-gene signature (Solyc09g074500, Solyc01g102960, Solyc07g042230, Solyc02g091990) achieved 98.5% classification accuracy and AUC > 0.95
comments: Rigorous multi-method integration (meta-analysis, WGCNA, SVM-RFE, LASSO) with high predictive performance, but functional validation in heat stress is absent.

hypotheses.hypothesis: A four-gene panel discriminates heat-stressed and control plants with high accuracy
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with 98.5% classification accuracy and individual AUCs > 0.95

title: Statistical models to characterize colon tumor stiffness heterogeneity through representative atomic force microscopy maps
task: Statistical modeling of tumor stiffness heterogeneity via atomic force microscopy maps
key_results: Identified novel associations between tissue stiffness and RAS mutations, tumor location, and microsatellite instability; ML inferred clinical parameters from stiffness data
comments: Integrative computational framework is solid but limited to colon cancer; causal links between stiffness properties and biological mechanisms remain inferred.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Tissue stiffness correlates with RAS mutations, tumor laterality, and DNA repair deficiency
hypotheses.result: Confirmed via statistical modeling

title: Exploring exosome-related genes as candidate biomarkers in primary immune thrombocytopenia through transcriptomics and preliminary experimental validation
task: Identification of exosome-related gene biomarkers in primary immune thrombocytopenia
key_results: Four candidate genes (GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, HIBADH, GSR) identified; three validated by RT-qPCR in ITP patient serum; molecular docking proposed drug candidates
comments: Exploratory bioinformatic-experimental integration with acknowledged limitations (immune infiltration analysis removed); HIBADH lacked experimental validation, weakening the four-gene panel.

model_io.model: Machine learning + pathway enrichment + molecular docking

outputs: GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, GSR expression (downregulated); HIBADH (not significant)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Exosome-related genes are dysregulated in ITP and associated with T-cell dysfunction
hypotheses.result: Three of four candidates validated; GABARAPL1, SLC39A14, GSR downregulated

title: Identification and verification of the key genes, CCR1 and EGR2, in diabetes-associated lipophagy
task: Identification of lipophagy-associated biomarkers in diabetes
key_results: CCR1 and EGR2 identified as hub genes; CCR1-/- mice showed improved glucose homeostasis under high-fat diet
comments: Multi-method convergence (WGCNA + machine learning) is solid; animal validation adds credibility but mechanistic link to lipophagy is inferred rather than directly demonstrated.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CCR1 and EGR2 are dysregulated in diabetes and associated with lipophagy
hypotheses.result: Both genes upregulated in diabetic samples; validated in serum and tissue

hypotheses.hypothesis: CCR1 deletion improves metabolic homeostasis
hypotheses.result: Confirmed in CCR1-/- mice under high-fat diet

title: A convolutional attention model classifies copy number variants from whole exome sequencing
task: Classification of copy number variants from whole-exome sequencing using convolutional attention neural network
key_results: CNN-Att achieved macro F1 = 0.83, macro PR-AUC = 0.93 on held-out test; cross-platform F1 up to 0.96 across four sequencing technologies
comments: Attention mechanism elegantly highlights weak depth signals for small events; robust cross-platform performance is valuable for clinical deployment, though training on only seven expert-annotated samples limits generalizability.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Attention-based CNN generalizes better across sequencing platforms than heuristic-based callers
hypotheses.result: Confirmed with F1 up to 0.96 across HiSeq 4000, NovaSeq 6000, MGISEQ 2000, BGISEQ 500

title: Optimization of cross-institutional medical federated learning framework driven by confidential computing
task: Optimization of federated learning with confidential computing and privacy-communication tradeoffs
key_results: CC-PGD reduces privacy leakage 23–31% and communication cost 18–27% vs baselines with O(1/√T) convergence guarantee
comments: First framework explicitly accounting for TEE side-channel vulnerabilities in federated learning; theoretical rigor is strong but real-world TEE threat modeling may be conservative.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Joint optimization of privacy and communication outperforms independent treatments
hypotheses.result: Confirmed on MNIST and CIFAR-10 under IID and non-IID partitioning

title: High-Resolution Downscaled CMIP6 Projections dataset of Key Climate Variables for Senegal
task: High-resolution climate projections dataset production and validation
key_results: 0.0375° × 0.0375° spatial resolution downscaled CMIP6 data for Senegal across 19 models, 1850–2100 for three SSP scenarios
comments: Primarily a data paper with statistical downscaling and quality control; limited novel methodological contribution.

inputs: CMIP6 global climate model outputs (19 models)
outputs: high-resolution daily climate variables (temp, precip, radiation)

title: Performance analysis of network automation techniques for dense IP networks
task: Network automation performance evaluation for dense IP networks
key_results: Automated topology creation (EVE-NG, Pllama, Container LAB) is 4.5× faster than manual; configuration generation automation ~10% faster than manual; test execution 11× faster than manual
comments: Empirical benchmark study; useful for practitioners but limited novelty in methodology or theoretical contribution.

model_io.model: Network automation tools (EVE-NG, Pllama, Container LAB, Nokia Komodo, Python, CLASSIC-CLI, MD-CLI, NETCONF)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Automation significantly reduces deployment time across all network stages
hypotheses.result: 4.5–11× speedup in topology creation, config generation, and testing vs. manual approaches

title: Sampled-data control under time-varying delays: a robust approach for high-renewable smart grids
task: Robust adaptive sampled-data control for high-renewable smart grids
key_results: Adaptive controller reduces settling time by 33%, overshoot by 52%, control energy cost by 40% vs. fixed-gain baselines under realistic communication impairments
comments: Rigorously certified via LMI conditions; effectively bridges communication-aware scheduling with control stability under bounded delay and jitter.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Real-time feedback gain scheduling based on communication quality improves stability under variable conditions
hypotheses.result: Exponential stability certified via Lyapunov methods; 33% faster settling time than worst-case designs

title: Research on the atomic clock signal denoising method based on the hyperbolic tangent smooth threshold function
task: Atomic clock signal denoising via hyperbolic tangent threshold
key_results: SNR improved 14% (cesium), 5% (hydrogen), 26% (rubidium); RMSE reduced 28%, 10%, 25% vs. soft thresholding
comments: Well-executed signal processing with empirical validation on three clock types, though the method combines known techniques (EMD, SURE, thresholding).

hypotheses.hypothesis: Smooth hyperbolic tangent threshold reduces discontinuity artifacts
hypotheses.result: Better SNR and RMSE than hard/soft thresholding while preserving signal fidelity

title: Adaptive feature selection with gradient-based relevance for intrusion detection systems
task: Adaptive feature selection for encrypted traffic intrusion detection
key_results: AFS improves detection accuracy 24.74% and reduces training time 35% vs. PCA-based methods on CIRA-CIC-DoHBrw-2020 dataset
comments: Incorporates gradient-based relevance for context-sensitive detection; practical gain for cybersecurity in energy systems.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Gradient-based relevance captures malicious patterns in encrypted traffic better than statistical relevance alone
hypotheses.result: 24.74% accuracy improvement and 35% training time reduction vs. PCA

title: Frequency-constrained robust unit commitment via physics-guided piecewise-linear nadir surrogates and adaptive virtual inertia
task: Frequency-constrained robust unit commitment with physics-guided surrogates
key_results: FC-RUC reduces total operating cost by 24.4% (IEEE 9-bus) and 27.5% (IEEE 118-bus) while satisfying all frequency-security constraints under N–k contingencies
comments: Physics-informed piecewise-linear surrogates for nadir constraints are elegant; column-and-constraint generation avoids full enumeration tractably.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-guided nadir surrogates + adaptive virtual inertia improve frequency resilience under high renewables
hypotheses.result: All frequency constraints satisfied; 24–27% cost reduction vs. conventional UC

title: Adaptive graph signal processing for robust multimodal fusion with dynamic semantic alignment
task: Multimodal fusion via adaptive graph signal processing with dynamic semantic alignment
key_results: AGSP-DSA achieves 95.3% accuracy and 93.6% F1 on CMU MOSEI (2.6% improvement over MultiModal GNN), 93.4% accuracy on Audio-Visual Event, 91.8% on MultiMM-IMDb
comments: Dual-graph construction for intra- and inter-modal relations is sensible; strong results across three benchmarks with robustness to missing modalities.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Dynamic semantic attention allows modalities to contribute contextually
hypotheses.result: Superior generalization and robustness in missing modality settings

title: AI-assisted reliability-based design framework for tunnel concrete linings in weak rocks
task: Reliability-based design of tunnel concrete linings using AI surrogate and CCM
key_results: Framework quantifies failure probability (Pf) and reliability index (β) for two lining types; FRPC (65 MPa) significantly reduces Pf at intermediate thicknesses vs. conventional concrete (20 MPa); surrogate model achieves high consistency with slight conservatism
comments: Well-integrated framework (CCM + Monte Carlo + FORM + AI surrogate) providing decision charts for risk-consistent design; practical utility for geotechnical engineering.

hypotheses.hypothesis: AI surrogate accurately reproduces probabilistic trends from CCM + Monte Carlo
hypotheses.result: Surrogate achieves high consistency with slight conservatism; enables rapid parametric analysis

hypotheses.hypothesis: FRPC (high strength) reduces Pf more efficiently than thickness increase alone
hypotheses.result: FRPC provides economical option when construction constraints limit thickness

title: Lightweight model LMW-YOLO for small object detection in remote sensing images
task: Small object detection in remote sensing images
key_results: Achieves state-of-the-art detection on NWPU VHR-10, RS-STOD, VisDrone2019 with only 2.6M parameters; SNR improvements of 14%, 5%, 26% on atomic clock data
comments: Strong practical contribution for resource-constrained aerial applications, though the atomic clock results seem misplaced in the abstract.

hypotheses.hypothesis: CSD strategy with LKCA and MSDP modules improves small target detection
hypotheses.result: Superior performance vs. state-of-the-art with minimal parameters

hypotheses.hypothesis: WIoU v3 loss accelerates convergence in dense scenarios
hypotheses.result: Enhanced localization accuracy through dynamic non-monotonic focusing

title: Multi-center chameleon hashing based Blockchain integrated digital copyright transaction scheme for data redacting in Blockchain based IoT systems
task: Blockchain-based digital copyright transaction with multi-center chameleon hashing
key_results: Scheme supports data redaction, flexible node management, and efficient private key broadcast in multi-node scenarios; satisfies confidentiality and malicious-node resistance
comments: Combines chameleon hashing with attribute encryption for BIoT/BIIOT; security analysis provided but limited quantitative performance metrics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Multi-center chameleon hashing enables privacy-preserving data redaction in blockchain
hypotheses.result: Confidentiality of transferor, resistance to malicious nodes, private key confidentiality achieved

title: Observation of partonic flow in proton—proton and proton—nucleus collisions
task: Observing quark-gluon plasma signatures in small collision systems
key_results: Distinctive baryon-meson flow grouping observed in proton-lead and proton-proton collisions; consistent with hydrodynamic flow and quark coalescence
comments: Extends QGP signatures beyond heavy-ion collisions to smaller systems, challenging prior assumptions about minimum system size; interpretation via quark coalescence is physically motivated but model-dependent.

inputs: proton-lead and proton-proton collisions at LHC

hypotheses.hypothesis: Quark-gluon plasma forms in high-multiplicity proton-proton and proton-lead collisions
hypotheses.result: Supported by observation of QGP-like flow patterns

title: Confined-hydrogel fluidic memristor crossbar array for neuromorphic computing
task: Scalable fluidic memristor array for neuromorphic computing
key_results: 10×10 confined hydrogel fluidic memristor array fabricated; 89.5% classification accuracy on MNIST handwritten digits; demonstrated neuromorphic behaviors (paired-pulse facilitation/depression, spike-rate-dependent plasticity)
comments: Novel gel-gel interface design enables scalability, but comparison with solid-state memristors on energy efficiency and speed is absent; practical deployment complexity remains high.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Confined hydrogel design enables scalable large arrays
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 10×10 array successfully fabricated

hypotheses.hypothesis: FMA can perform neuromorphic image recognition
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 89.5% MNIST accuracy achieved

title: Physics-informed Hamiltonian learning for large-scale optoelectronic property prediction
task: Physics-informed machine learning for optoelectronic property prediction in large-scale systems
key_results: HAMSTER framework accurately predicts quantum Hamiltonians and optoelectronic properties across temperature/compositional variations; scalable to ~10,000 atom systems with few first-principles calculations
comments: Hybrid physics-ML approach balances interpretability and data efficiency; demonstration on halide perovskites is relevant, but transferability to other material classes remains to be tested.

outputs: optoelectronic properties (bandgap, exciton binding energy, etc.)

hypotheses.hypothesis: Physics-informed approximate models require fewer data points than pure ML
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: high accuracy achieved with minimal first-principles reference data

title: Orders-of-magnitude improvement in precision spectroscopy of an inner-shell orbital clock transition in neutral ytterbium
task: Precision spectroscopy of inner-shell clock transition in ytterbium for fundamental physics tests
key_results: ~100-fold improvement in spectroscopy precision; isotope shift measurements below 10-Hz uncertainty; constraints on hypothetical electron-neutron mediating boson
comments: Magic-wavelength lattice trapping elegantly enables access to previously inaccessible precision; results are fundamental for dark matter and Lorentz invariance searches.

model_io.model: Yb atoms in 3D magic-wavelength optical lattice
inputs: optical probe at inner-shell transition wavelength

hypotheses.hypothesis: Magic-wavelength lattice significantly improves spectroscopic precision
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: ~100× improvement over prior work

hypotheses.hypothesis: Boson-mediated electron-neutron force exists at measurable strength
hypotheses.result: Bounds set; no evidence found within sensitivity limits

title: Exceptional sensitivity near the bistable transition point of a hybrid quantum system
task: Enhanced magnetometry using bistable transition points in hybrid quantum systems
key_results: 17-fold signal-to-noise ratio enhancement in nitrogen-vacancy magnetometry near bistable transition; surpassed ideal bare electron magnetometer sensitivity
comments: Clever exploitation of nonlinearity to overcome the noise amplification problem at linear exceptional points, though practical applicability to portable devices remains unclear.

model_io.model: NV-center coupled to van der Pol oscillator

hypotheses.hypothesis: Nonlinearity at bistable transition provides better SNR than linear exceptional points
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 17-fold enhancement achieved

title: First positronium lifetime imaging using 52Mn and 55Co with a plastic-scintillator-based PET scanner
task: Positronium lifetime imaging using 52Mn and 55Co positron emitters
key_results: First PLI measurements with 52Mn and 55Co on J-PET; τ_oPs values in polycarbonate match certified references; 55Co provides cleaner data than 52Mn despite longer half-life of latter
comments: Technical demonstration of isotope viability for PLI; results are reference-material confirmatory but tissue studies show age-related degradation rather than isotope differences; clinical applicability requires fresh tissue validation.

model_io.model: J-PET modular scanner with 52Mn and 55Co isotopes
inputs: positron emitters in test samples (polycarbonate, fused silica, cardiac tissue, adipose tissue)

hypotheses.hypothesis: 52Mn and 55Co are suitable for positronium lifetime imaging
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: both isotopes produced interpretable PLI spectra

hypotheses.hypothesis: 55Co provides superior data quality to 52Mn in PLI
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: cleaner peak-to-background ratio and spectral purity with 55Co

title: Mirrorless open cavities enabled by boundary incompatibility between perfect electric conductor and perfect magnetic conductor parallel-plate waveguides
task: Electromagnetic confinement without mirrors via PEC-PMC boundary incompatibility
key_results: Quality factor Q~10² in open cavity; strong field localization and enhanced magnetic-dipole Purcell factor; functional despite Ohmic and dielectric losses
comments: Elegant demonstration of mode-gap confinement; open geometry is valuable for coupling to biological/quantum systems, but practical scalability to desired frequency ranges and broadband operation needs exploration.

model_io.model: PEC-PMC waveguide cavity with metallic-groove artificial magnetic conductor

hypotheses.hypothesis: Mode gap at PEC-PMC interface confines EM waves without physical mirrors
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: sharp transmission resonance with Q~10² observed

title: Study on the deoxygenation characteristics of RP-5 aviation fuel via nitrogen-enriched air scrubbing inerting
task: Oxygen removal from aviation fuel via nitrogen-enriched air scrubbing inerting
key_results: Reducing bubble diameter from 2.5 mm to 1.0 mm increases oxygen volumetric mass transfer coefficient ~3.9×; temperature exhibits competing effects between kinetics and thermodynamic solubility
comments: Systematic CFD + experimental study reveals practical optimization strategies; temperature effects are nuanced and counterintuitive, providing useful design guidance for airborne inerting systems.

model_io.model: Eulerian-Eulerian two-fluid CFD model with gas-liquid mass transfer

hypotheses.hypothesis: Reduced bubble diameter significantly enhances oxygen mass transfer
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: 3.9× coefficient increase for 2.5→1.0 mm reduction

hypotheses.hypothesis: Higher temperature improves deoxygenation performance
hypotheses.result: Contradicted: elevated temperature worsens final performance due to increased gas solubility

title: A single diode with integrated photosensing, memory and processing for neuromorphic image sensors
task: Multi-functional image sensor via band-structure engineered photodiode with integrated sensing, memory, and processing
key_results: p-GaN/n-AlGaN/n-GaN nanowire diode exhibits responsivity 10.45 mA/W, paired-pulse facilitation ratio up to 122%, eight linear memory states; array performs image denoising and classification without additional circuits
comments: Elegant single-device integration via band engineering eliminates multi-terminal complexity; practical advantages in size and power consumption are clear, but pixel integration density and manufacturing yield at scale need validation.

model_io.model: p-GaN/n-AlGaN/n-GaN nanowire p-n photodiode with embedded electron reservoir

hypotheses.hypothesis: AlGaN bandgap engineering creates tunable electron reservoir for multi-function operation
hypotheses.result: Confirmed: integrated photosensing, memory, and processing demonstrated in single device
